pilot_notes: CHT #3 running ~30°F above other cylinders in cruise — 395°F vs 365°F others at 8500ft. Filed squawk SQ-2024-CHT3.
Spark plugs cleaned after squawk — CHT equal all cylinders. SQ-2024-CHT3 resolved.
DA ~6500ft at KFLG. Long ground roll on 21. CHT peaked 415°F on climb, normalized to 385°F at cruise altitude (9500ft). Density altitude effects notable — performance reduced as expected.
OAT 106°F on ground at KPHX. Oil temp peaked 214°F on initial climb, normal at cruise altitude. CHT normal.
July 4 morning flight — OAT 98°F. All normal.
Fuel odor in cockpit on climbout — right fuel cap seal. Filed SQ-2024-FUELCAP.
New fuel cap installed — no fuel odor. SQ-2024-FUELCAP resolved.
OAT 108°F on ground. Oil temp peaked 218°F on initial climb — within limits (245°F redline). Normal at cruise altitude. CHT 398°F on climb.
DA ~6200ft at KFLG. CHT peaked 412°F on climb. Excellent mountain visibility — ATIS reported 50sm vis.
Grand Canyon fall trip. DA ~5800ft at KGCN. Long flight — spectacular visibility. CHT normalized at 9500ft cruise.
DA ~6800ft at KFLG — summer density altitude significant. Long ground roll on Rwy 21. CHT peaked 410°F climb, good at cruise.
Grand Canyon summer trip. OAT 102°F KPHX departure. Oil temp peaked 216°F initial climb — within limits. CHT 396°F climb at KGCN altitude. Spectacular monsoon cloud formations around the canyon.
OAT 112°F on ground. Oil temp 218°F peak on climb — warm but within limits. No anomalies.
First flight after annual and oil change. All systems normal.
Flagstaff winter flight — cool and smooth. DA ~5200ft.
First flight after Dec 15 oil change.
Nice winter Flagstaff trip. DA ~5000ft. All normal.
Spring Grand Canyon trip. Perfect VFR day.
